description: Authored grammar corpus — the shared contract between two tests. (1) The drift test in packages/malloy/src/lang/test/ tokenizes each `code` with the real MalloyLexer and asserts every `expect[].text` span maps to a token of the claimed `lexer` kind — catching 'the language moved'. (2) The hermetic grammar test in this package tokenizes each `code` with vscode-textmate and asserts every `expect[].text` span carries the claimed `scope`. `lexer` is verified ground truth (run scripts/oracle); `scope` is an authored design decision. Neither test is generated; this file is the spec.
scopeConventions: Theme-portable TextMate scopes only — no borrowed .json/.ts suffixes. Themes key off scope prefixes (string, variable, constant, keyword, punctuation), so the trailing .malloy is specificity for our own theme rules, not a coloring dependency.

cases.name: filter literal, single quote (f-prefix) — UNSUPPORTED today
filter literal, triple single quote (f-prefix) — UNSUPPORTED today
filter literal, double quote (f-prefix) — UNSUPPORTED today
SQL block: a """ string as an argument to connection.sql(...) IS SQL
cases.note: SQL-ness comes from the .sql() context, NOT the """ delimiter. The lexer cannot know this (it just sees SQL_BEGIN); the grammar must trigger SQL off the .sql( context.

cases.name: SQL block: a SHORT string in connection.sql(...) is ALSO SQL
cases.note: Lexer sees only SQ_STRING (a plain string) — false negative. Any string in .sql() is SQL; only the .sql() context tells you so.

cases.name: bare """ is a MULTI-LINE STRING, not SQL
cases.note: Lexer fires SQL_BEGIN/SQL_END here too — false positive. Outside .sql(), """ is just a multi-line string literal. lexer != scope: the drift test checks the boundary, the scope is authored from parser semantics.
cases.code:
dimension: doc is """line one
line two"""

cases.name: interpolation %{...} INSIDE a .sql() block — valid; closes on plain } (today's }%? end pattern is wrong)
%{...} inside a BARE """ string is NOT an interpolation — plain string text
cases.note: Compiler raises illegal-query-interpolation-outside-sql-block. The lexer still emits OPEN_CODE/CCURLY (blind to context); the highlighter must keep the whole span as string and NOT treat %{ } as an embedded section. This is the case TextMate gets right by giving the bare-""" rule no interpolation pattern.

cases.name: annotation: bare object tag
cases.note: The lexer makes the whole line one ANNOTATION token — tag CONTENT is not sub-lexed (the tag parser does that). So drift assertions on tag parts all map to ANNOTATION; the grammar is what sub-divides into marker / tag-name / value.

cases.name: annotation: key=value (bare value shares the tag-name scope in v1)
annotation: quoted value is a string
##internal (no space) — parsePrefix routes to a malformed bare-word route; highlighter still colors content as a tag (it does not diagnose)
block object annotation #| ... |# (column-matched close)
cases.note: Lexer emits BLOCK_ANNOTATION_BEGIN / _TEXT / _END; the column match is approximated in TextMate by a leading-whitespace backreference. v1 applies tag-content to block bodies, so prose used as a comment is colored as tags — flagged as a possible future muting decision.

cases.name: documentation route #" — NOT a tag, styled like documentation
cases.note: Per docs.malloydata.dev/documentation/language/tags, #" is the Explorer-description route; content is markdown/prose, not tag property-language. Lexer still emits one ANNOTATION token (the route is a tag-parser concern); the grammar distinguishes #" from # to style content as documentation, NOT entity.name.tag. Word 'tag' chosen in the text to prove it is NOT colored as a tag.
cases.code: #" This is a tag-free documentation line

cases.name: bracketed app route #(name) — content is app-defined, styled as documentation
cases.note: parsePrefix routes #(viz) to the opaque app route 'viz' (brackets stripped, no malformation). Content format is app-defined (JSON/markdown/...), so v1 styles it as documentation, not tags. Word 'config' proves it is not tag-colored.

cases.name: block with app route #|(name) … |# — routes apply to blocks too
cases.note: The block | is part of the sigil; the route follows it. A bracketed route makes the whole block documentation, not tags — even though the empty-route #| block above is tags. This is the case that proves block forms are routed.

cases.name: keyword coverage: json type
cases.note: Newly added keywords (json/this/condition/has/virtual/calculation) were uncolored before. Scopes match each section's existing convention (no .malloy suffix — that blanket rename is deferred).

cases.name: colon-keyword in field position is NOT a keyword (the (?=:) win)
cases.note: group_by: carries its colon → keyword. The bare `query` after it is a field reference — the lexer emits IDENTIFIER, not QUERY (which requires a colon) — so it must be a variable, not a keyword. This is exactly the 'bad thing' a per-word match caused; the (?=:) lookahead fixes it while leaving the colon uncolored.
